---
title: "Installation"
---

## System Requirements

- Linux OS (e.g., CentOS, Ubuntu, Debian, etc.)
- ~590 GB disk space for software and reference databases
- For optimal performance: 32 CPU cores with 3 GB RAM per core (96 GB total). SC16 (*de novo* tree generation) may require >100 GB RAM.

## Dependencies

- [Git](https://git-scm.com/)
- [Apptainer](https://apptainer.org/)
- [Bash](https://tiswww.case.edu/php/chet/bash/bashtop.html) as command-line shell
- All further software dependencies are bundled in Apptainer images, automatically downloaded during installation. This ensures:
  1. **Ease of use** — simplified installation and execution
  2. **Portability** — consistent performance across computing environments
  3. **Reproducibility** — identical software environments for all users
  4. **Version control** — precise management of software versions
  5. **Isolation** — minimal conflicts with existing system libraries

## Default Installation (Interactive)

Clone the repository:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/SBUJordi/StrainCascade.git
```

Navigate to the StrainCascade directory:

```bash
cd StrainCascade
```

Make all scripts executable:

```bash
find scripts/ -type f -exec chmod +x {} \;
```

Run the installation script:

```bash
# Interactive installation (recommended for first-time users)
./scripts/StrainCascade_installation.sh
```

::: {.callout-note}
The installation may take between 12 hours to over a day, depending on your system and network speed. Large database downloads (GTDB-Tk, MicrobeAnnotator, Bakta) are the longest steps.
:::

If you choose the default interactive installation, you will be prompted:

```
Running StrainCascade installation...
Sufficient disk space available: XXXGB
Apptainer is installed.
Do you want to do a full installation of StrainCascade? (y/n)
```

## Non-Interactive Installation

For automated / unattended installs (CI, Dockerfile, HPC batch jobs):

```bash
# Idempotent full install — safe to re-run after a crash
./scripts/StrainCascade_installation.sh --full --yes

# Force reinstall everything
./scripts/StrainCascade_installation.sh --full --yes --force

# Quiet install (output goes to a log file under apptainer_images/.install_logs/)
STRAINCASCADE_VERBOSITY=0 ./scripts/StrainCascade_installation.sh --full --yes

# Verbose install (full subcommand output)
STRAINCASCADE_VERBOSITY=2 ./scripts/StrainCascade_installation.sh --full --yes
```

Available flags:

| Flag | Effect |
|---|---|
| `--full` | Run all phases (PATH + images + databases). Implies `--skip-existing` unless `--force` is given. |
| `--scripts` | PATH setup only. |
| `--images` | Pull Apptainer images only. |
| `--databases` | Install databases only. |
| `--skip-existing` | Skip already-installed components (idempotent re-runs). |
| `--force` | Force reinstall, overwriting any existing data. |
| `-y`, `--yes` | Auto-accept all prompts. |
| `-h`, `--help` | Show help. |

`STRAINCASCADE_VERBOSITY` accepts `0` (quiet, log-only), `1` (normal, default), or `2` (verbose).

## Running the Installation on an HPC Cluster

The full installation downloads ~500 GB and takes 12–30 h, so it should be submitted as a batch job rather than run on a login node. A scheduler-agnostic SLURM template is provided at [`scripts/install.sbatch.template`](https://github.com/SBUJordi/StrainCascade/blob/main/scripts/install.sbatch.template). Copy it, edit the site-specific placeholders (`--account`, `--partition`, `--qos`, e-mail), and submit from the StrainCascade root:

```bash
cp scripts/install.sbatch.template install.sbatch
# edit the #SBATCH header for your cluster, then:
sbatch install.sbatch
```

Recommended resources (validated on a typical HPC node):

| Resource | Value | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| CPUs | 8 | MicrobeAnnotator parallel downloads; further parallelism gives no speed-up because the bottleneck is remote FTP. |
| Memory | 96 GB | MicrobeAnnotator peaks around 50 GB resident memory when parsing the RefSeq–KO mapping. |
| Walltime | 48 h | Typical end-to-end runtime is 12–30 h; the extra margin accommodates network fluctuations. |
| Local scratch | not required | The installer redirects Apptainer `TMPDIR`/`CACHEDIR` to the installation filesystem and forces `TMPDIR=/tmp` inside containers, so node-local scratch is not needed. |

The submitted job runs `StrainCascade_installation.sh --full --yes --skip-existing` and is safe to resubmit unchanged if it fails or hits the wall time: only missing components will be re-attempted.

PBS / LSF / SGE users can reuse the body of the template and translate the `#SBATCH` directives to their scheduler's syntax.

## Custom Installation

Run the installer interactively and answer `n` when asked for a full installation; the installer will then offer each component individually:

```
Do you want to install checkm2_db? (y/n)
```

Useful when you have limited disk space or bandwidth, or when you want to install just one component.

## Resuming a Failed Installation

Database downloads (especially MicrobeAnnotator, GTDB-Tk, Bakta) can fail after many hours due to remote-server hiccups. The installer is designed to be **idempotent**: simply re-run with `--skip-existing` and only the missing/incomplete pieces will be installed:

```bash
./scripts/StrainCascade_installation.sh --full --yes --skip-existing
```

Failure logs are written under `apptainer_images/.install_logs/`.

## Updating StrainCascade

StrainCascade provides separate update mechanisms for its three main components:

```bash
# Update software scripts (uses 'git pull --ff-only' if installed via git)
straincascade -us

# Update Apptainer images
straincascade -uai

# Update databases
straincascade -udb
```

::: {.callout-tip}
These individual update mechanisms are designed for minor version updates and patches. Major version releases with significant new features may require a complete reinstallation.
:::

## Caveats

- **PATH configuration:** The installer prints the exact `export PATH=...` line you need and asks before modifying any shell init file. With `--yes` it auto-appends to the rc file matching your `$SHELL`. If you maintain multiple installs, verify `which straincascade` points to the one you intend.

- **Third-party database downloads:** Larger databases (GTDB-Tk, MicrobeAnnotator, Bakta, AMRFinderPlus) may occasionally fail due to network or remote-server issues. The installer now retries with exponential backoff and supports resuming via `--skip-existing`. If you still hit a hard failure, retry after a few hours.

- **Managing Apptainer images after updates:** Newer image versions may be downloaded during updates. StrainCascade prioritises the first matching image. Check and clean the `apptainer_images` directory after re-installations:

  ```bash
  cd $(dirname $(which straincascade))/../apptainer_images
  ```

- **Advanced — running multiple installations side by side:** If you want each installation isolated, activate a dedicated Conda environment (`conda create -n StrainCascade_env && conda activate StrainCascade_env`) before running the installer. The installer detects the active env and scopes `PATH` to it via `activate.d`/`deactivate.d` hooks. For a single global installation this is not needed.
